title: Would Like To — Polite Wishes & Requests

sections.title: Introduction & Formula — Would Like To
sections.hindiTitle: परिचय और फॉर्मूला — Would Like To
sections.explanation: 'Would like to' English का एक बहुत important और polite structure है। यह 'want to' का formal/polite version है। Daily life में, office में, restaurant में, interview में — हर जगह यह use होता है। Formula बहुत simple है: Subject + would like + to + base verb। Contraction: I'd like to / She'd like to / He'd like to / We'd like to / They'd like to। सभी subjects के साथ 'would' same रहता है — कोई changes नहीं होता। उदाहरण: I would like to meet the manager. (मैं manager से मिलना चाहूँगा।) यह 'I want to meet the manager' से ज़्यादा polite है। Key difference: 'want' = casual/informal; 'would like' = polite/formal।
sections.formula: Subject + would like + to + base verb | Contraction: I'd/You'd/He'd/She'd/We'd/They'd + like to + base verb

examples.hindi: मैं एक कप चाय लेना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to have a cup of tea.

examples.hindi: वह manager से मिलना चाहेगी।
examples.english: She would like to meet the manager.

examples.hindi: हम इस project पर काम करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We would like to work on this project.

examples.hindi: क्या आप कुछ खाना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to eat something?

examples.hindi: मैं आपसे एक बात पूछना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I'd like to ask you something.

examples.hindi: वे अगले महीने यात्रा करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: They would like to travel next month.

sections.title: Daily Life Usage — Would Like To
sections.hindiTitle: रोज़मर्रा की ज़िंदगी में Would Like To
sections.explanation: Daily life में 'would like to' बहुत naturally use होता है — घर पर, दोस्तों के साथ, shopping करते समय, restaurant में। यह आपकी बात को polite और educated बनाता है। Restaurant में: 'I would like to order a coffee.' दोस्त से: 'I'd like to go to the movies tonight.' Family में: 'I would like to visit grandma this Sunday.' Shopping में: 'I would like to try this shirt.' इन sentences को याद करें और daily life में use करें।
sections.formula: Subject + would like to + base verb (daily activities)

examples.hindi: मैं आज रात बाहर खाना खाना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to eat out tonight.

examples.hindi: क्या आप मेरे साथ coffee पीना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to have coffee with me?

examples.hindi: वह नई movie देखना चाहेगा।
examples.english: He would like to watch the new movie.

examples.hindi: हम weekend पर picnic करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We would like to go on a picnic this weekend.

examples.hindi: मैं यह किताब पढ़ना चाहूँगी।
examples.english: I would like to read this book.

examples.hindi: वे नए घर में shift होना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: They would like to move to a new house.

sections.title: Office & Professional Usage — Would Like To
sections.hindiTitle: ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग — Would Like To
sections.explanation: Office और professional settings में 'would like to' बहुत ज़रूरी है। यह आपको polished और professional बनाता है। Email में: 'I would like to schedule a meeting.' Meeting में: 'I would like to add one more point.' Client को: 'We would like to offer you our best service.' Boss को: 'I would like to discuss my performance review.' Interview में: 'I would like to contribute to your team.' यह structure job interviews में बहुत अच्छा impression बनाता है। हमेशा 'want' की जगह 'would like to' use करें professional contexts में।
sections.formula: Subject + would like to + base verb (professional context)

examples.hindi: मैं इस meeting में एक point add करना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to add a point in this meeting.

examples.hindi: क्या आप इस proposal को review करना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to review this proposal?

examples.hindi: हम आपके साथ partnership करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We would like to partner with you.

examples.hindi: वह अपनी salary बढ़वाने की बात करना चाहेगा।
examples.english: He would like to discuss a salary raise.

examples.hindi: मैं इस नए role के लिए apply करना चाहूँगी।
examples.english: I would like to apply for this new role.

examples.hindi: वे अगले quarter में expansion करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: They would like to expand next quarter.

sections.title: Negative & Question Forms — Would Like To
sections.hindiTitle: नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप — Would Like To
sections.explanation: Negative form: Subject + would not like to + base verb। Contraction: wouldn't like to। उदाहरण: I wouldn't like to be rude. / She would not like to miss this opportunity. Question form: Would + subject + like to + base verb? उदाहरण: Would you like to join us? / Would he like to speak first? Short answers: Yes, I would. / No, I wouldn't. Offering something: Would you like to have some water? Asking preference: Would you like to sit here or there? याद रखें — question में 'would' subject से पहले आता है।
sections.formula: Negative: Subject + wouldn't/would not + like to + base verb | Question: Would + subject + like to + base verb?

examples.hindi: मैं देर तक काम नहीं करना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I wouldn't like to work late today.

examples.hindi: क्या वह इस team में join होना चाहेगा?
examples.english: Would he like to join this team?

examples.hindi: वह इस topic पर बात नहीं करना चाहेगी।
examples.english: She wouldn't like to talk about this topic.

examples.hindi: क्या आप पानी पीना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to have some water?

examples.hindi: हम अपना समय बर्बाद नहीं करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We wouldn't like to waste our time.

examples.hindi: क्या वे presentation देना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would they like to give the presentation?

sections.title: Common Mistakes with Would Like To
sections.hindiTitle: Would Like To में आम गलतियाँ
sections.explanation: ❌ WRONG: I would like eating. ✅ RIGHT: I would like to eat. (हमेशा 'to' + base verb लगाएं, never gerund/ing form directly after 'like'।) ❌ WRONG: I would likes to go. ✅ RIGHT: I would like to go. ('would' के बाद 'like' — कोई 's' नहीं।) ❌ WRONG: I would like to went. ✅ RIGHT: I would like to go. ('to' के बाद हमेशा base verb — past/past participle नहीं।) ❌ WRONG: Does she would like to come? ✅ RIGHT: Would she like to come? (Question बनाने के लिए 'would' को subject से पहले लाएं, 'does' नहीं।) ❌ WRONG: I am liking to go. ✅ RIGHT: I would like to go. ('Would like' एक fixed expression है — continuous form नहीं होता।)
sections.formula: CORRECT: Subject + would + like to + base verb (NOT: would likes / would like + ing / did would like)

examples.hindi: ❌ मैं जाना लाइक करूँगा। ✅ मैं जाना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: ❌ I would like going. ✅ I would like to go.

examples.hindi: ❌ क्या वह जाना चाहती है? (using 'does') ✅ क्या वह जाना चाहेगी?
examples.english: ❌ Does she would like to go? ✅ Would she like to go?

examples.hindi: मुझे coffee पीना है — politely कहना है।
examples.english: I would like to have a coffee. (NOT: I would like having a coffee.)

examples.hindi: उसे यह जानना है — politely।
examples.english: He would like to know this. (NOT: He would likes to know.)

examples.hindi: क्या तुम आना चाहते हो — formal form।
examples.english: Would you like to come? (NOT: Do you would like to come?)

examples.hindi: हम meeting reschedule करना चाहते हैं — politely।
examples.english: We would like to reschedule the meeting. (NOT: We would like rescheduling.)

sections.title: Formal & Customer Service Contexts
sections.hindiTitle: Formal और Customer Service में Would Like To
sections.explanation: Customer service, hospitality, और formal business में 'would like to' हर जगह होता है। Hotel receptionist: 'Would you like to check in now?' Waiter: 'Would you like to see the menu?' Bank employee: 'I would like to open a savings account.' Call center: 'I would like to speak to the manager please.' Shop assistant: 'Would you like to try this in a different size?' HR: 'We would like to offer you the position.' यह formal register में 'want' से कहीं ज़्यादा appropriate है। Customers/clients को हमेशा 'would like to' use करके address करें।
sections.formula: Would you like to + base verb? (offering) | I would like to + base verb. (requesting)

examples.hindi: क्या आप menu देखना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to see the menu?

examples.hindi: मैं एक savings account खोलना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to open a savings account.

examples.hindi: क्या आप दूसरा size try करना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to try a different size?

examples.hindi: हम आपको यह position offer करना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We would like to offer you this position.

examples.hindi: मैं manager से बात करना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to speak to the manager.

examples.hindi: क्या आप room upgrade लेना चाहेंगे?
examples.english: Would you like to upgrade your room?

sections.title: Comparison: Would Like To vs Want To vs Would Love To
sections.hindiTitle: तुलना: Would Like To बनाम Want To बनाम Would Love To
sections.explanation: 'Want to' = casual/informal (दोस्तों के बीच, घर पर): I want to eat pizza. 'Would like to' = polite/formal (office, interview, strangers से बात करते वक्त): I would like to have a meal. 'Would love to' = enthusiastic/very willing (किसी invitation को खुशी से accept करना): I would love to attend your wedding! Politeness scale: want < would like < would love। Interview tip: हमेशा 'would like to' या 'would love to' use करें, कभी भी सिर्फ 'want' नहीं। 'Would prefer to' भी similar है लेकिन यह choice/preference दर्शाता है: I would prefer to work from home.
sections.formula: Informal: want to | Polite: would like to | Enthusiastic: would love to | Preference: would prefer to

examples.hindi: मैं pizza खाना चाहता हूँ। (casual)
examples.english: I want to eat pizza. (casual/informal)

examples.hindi: मैं dinner करना चाहूँगा। (polite)
examples.english: I would like to have dinner. (polite/formal)

examples.hindi: मैं आपकी शादी में आना चाहूँगा! (enthusiastic)
examples.english: I would love to attend your wedding!

examples.hindi: मैं घर से काम करना prefer करूँगा।
examples.english: I would prefer to work from home.

examples.hindi: वह यहाँ रहना चाहेगी। (polite)
examples.english: She would like to stay here. (polite)

examples.hindi: वे इस project में शामिल होना चाहेंगे! (eager)
examples.english: They would love to be part of this project!

sections.title: Practice Tips & Advanced Usage
sections.explanation: Advanced usage — 'Would like to' को और natural बनाने के tips: 1. 'I'd' (contraction) use करें — यह ज़्यादा natural sounds: I'd like to know more. 2. Indirect speech में: He said he would like to meet the director. 3. Conditional के साथ: If possible, I would like to work from home. 4. Formal email में: I would like to bring to your attention... / I would like to request a meeting. 5. 'Would like + noun' भी possible है (without 'to'): I would like a glass of water. (to drink implicitly understood) 6. Practice tip: रोज़ 5 situations लिखें जहाँ आप would like to use करेंगे — restaurant, office, interview, phone call, email।
sections.formula: Advanced: I'd like to... | Indirect: He said he would like to... | Formal email: I would like to bring to your attention...

examples.hindi: अगर मुमकिन हो, तो मैं घर से काम करना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: If possible, I'd like to work from home.

examples.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह director से मिलना चाहेगा।
examples.english: He said he would like to meet the director.

examples.hindi: मैं आपका ध्यान इस बात की ओर लाना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I would like to bring this to your attention.

examples.hindi: एक glass पानी मिल सकता है?
examples.english: I would like a glass of water, please.

examples.hindi: मैं आपसे meeting schedule करना चाहूँगा।
examples.english: I'd like to schedule a meeting with you.

examples.hindi: हम आपको हमारी नई service के बारे में बताना चाहेंगे।
examples.english: We would like to inform you about our new service.
